summary: FTS5-only baseline; OR-of-quoted-tokens query builder, drops words <3 chars and basic stopwords
expt#1: add auxiliary stopwords (has, been, shown, can, etc) to query builder; killed false-positive matches on the NAD question
expt#1: add auxiliary stopwords (has, been, shown, can, etc) to query builder
expt#2: AND-of-discriminators with OR fallback; no delta. Pure FTS5 has hit its ceiling on the caloric-restriction question (zero shared keywords)

expt#3: ARCHITECTURAL — added GeminiEmbedder + cosine dense retrieval + RRF fusion of dense+lexical. R@1: 0.80 (FTS) → 0.90 (FTS+stopwords) → 1.00 (hybrid). The caloric restriction question (zero shared keywords) is now solved by Gemini Embed 2.
fix: deterministic tiebreak + embedding cache; honest hybrid baseline is 0.9 (previous 1.0 was lucky non-determinism). This is the new floor.
expt#5: deterministic UUIDs + fusion tiebreak. R@1 = 1.0 hybrid_gemini stably across 5 runs. Mini bench is now SATURATED — next: expand test set or wire LongMemEval.

MILESTONE: hybrid_gemini on LongMemEval S first 50 questions: R@1=0.90 R@5=0.98 R@10=1.00 MRR=0.94. R@5 matches MemPalace hybrid_v4 (0.984). No reranking, no graph, no AAAK — just FTS5 + Gemini + RRF.
MILESTONE: hybrid_gemini on LongMemEval S first 100 questions: R@1=0.89 R@5=0.98 R@10=1.00 MRR=0.94. R@5 holds at MemPalace parity at 100Q scale.
